title: Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors
subtitle: The estimated share of deaths from [cancers](#dod:cancer) attributed to all [risk factors](#dod:risk-factor). The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.
caption:
Share of cancer deaths attributed to risk factors, World, 2023

The estimated share of deaths from cancers attributed to all risk factors. The remaining share represents deaths that would be expected to have occurred in the absence of these known risk factors.

This horizontal bar chart ranks cancer types by the percentage of global deaths in 2023 attributed to known risk factors, with values ranging from 0.9% to 100%. Cervical cancer sits at the top of the distribution with 100% of deaths linked to risk factors, followed by mesothelioma at 91.7% and lung, tracheal and bronchus cancers at 76.4%. Larynx cancer registers 73.4%, while esophageal and colon and rectum cancers both fall in the mid-50% range at 56.4% and 56.2% respectively. Liver cancer shows 48.2% of deaths attributed to risk factors, with lip and oral cavity cancers at 39.4% and pancreatic cancers at 38.7%. Uterine and bladder cancers both register around one-third of deaths at 33.3% and 33%, while breast and kidney cancers fall in the high 20% range at 29.8% and 27.9%. Nasopharynx cancer shows 23.7%, stomach cancer 19%, and leukemia 18.7%. Thyroid and gallbladder and biliary tract cancers both register 11.6% and 11.4% respectively. Multiple myeloma shows 7.6% of deaths attributed to risk factors, non-Hodgkin lymphoma 4.8%, and prostate cancer at the bottom of the ranking with 0.9%. The bars show a steep gradient from cervical cancer at the maximum down through the middle range cancers, with the lowest values concentrated among prostate, non-Hodgkin lymphoma, and multiple myeloma.
